item_a.name: Casper Original Mattress
item_a.image_alt: Casper Original all-foam mattress with zoned support layers on white background
pros: Excellent pressure relief with three-layer foam construction
cons: Can sleep warmer than hybrid alternatives
Edge support is weaker than innerspring models
Off-gassing odor during first few days
item_a.best_for: Side sleepers and couples who prioritize motion isolation and pressure relief in a convenient all-foam package.

item_b.name: Saatva Classic Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Saatva Classic luxury innerspring mattress with Euro pillow top and dual coil system
pros: Dual-coil construction provides excellent support and durability
Three firmness options (Plush Soft, Luxury Firm, Firm)
Superior edge support and bounce from innerspring design
Free white glove delivery and old mattress removal included
cons: Higher price point than bed-in-a-box competitors
More motion transfer than all-foam mattresses
Heavier weight (80-140 lbs) makes repositioning difficult
item_b.best_for: Back and stomach sleepers seeking a traditional innerspring feel with luxury hotel-style comfort and premium service.

comparison_summary: The Casper vs Saatva debate centers on foam convenience versus innerspring tradition. Casper delivers excellent pressure relief and motion isolation in an affordable, easy-to-setup package, while Saatva offers superior support, edge stability, and breathability with a luxury innerspring construction at nearly twice the price. Your sleep position and budget will determine the winner.
verdict_text: Saatva Classic wins for most sleepers due to superior durability, support, and temperature regulation, though Casper remains the better value for budget-conscious side sleepers.

key_differences.item_b: Dual-coil hybrid (recycled steel coils + memory foam lumbar pad)
Three options: Plush Soft (4/10), Luxury Firm (6/10), Firm (8/10)
Moderate - some motion transfer due to coils
Excellent - foam-encased perimeter with 8-9 inch usable edge

key_differences.item_a: Moderate - perforated foam but retains some heat
key_differences.item_b: Excellent - breathable coil system with organic cotton cover
White glove delivery with setup and old mattress removal

seo_content:
When comparing Casper vs Saatva, you're evaluating two fundamentally different approaches to mattress design. Casper pioneered the bed-in-a-box revolution with its all-foam Original mattress, while Saatva built its reputation on delivering traditional innerspring luxury at direct-to-consumer prices. Understanding these core differences is essential to making the right choice for your sleep needs.

The Casper Original features a three-layer all-foam construction with zoned support designed to cradle pressure points while maintaining spinal alignment. Its memory foam comfort layer excels at motion isolation, making it ideal for couples where one partner tosses and turns. The medium-firm feel (around 6 out of 10) suits most side sleepers and combination sleepers, though stomach sleepers may want additional support. At $695-$1,395 depending on size, Casper represents excellent value in the foam mattress category.

Saatva vs Casper reveals a different philosophy. The Saatva Classic uses a dual-coil system with individually wrapped comfort coils atop a supportive base layer of tempered steel coils. This construction provides exceptional edge support, superior breathability, and the responsive bounce many sleepers prefer. The lumbar zone enhancement with memory foam adds targeted lower back support. Saatva's three firmness levels (Plush Soft, Luxury Firm, and Firm) accommodate a wider range of sleep preferences than Casper's single option. However, this premium construction comes at $1,395-$2,695, nearly double Casper's price.

Temperature regulation strongly favors Saatva. The coil-on-coil design allows excellent airflow, while Casper's foam layers can trap heat despite perforations. Hot sleepers consistently rate Saatva cooler. Conversely, motion isolation clearly favors Casper—its foam absorbs movement better than Saatva's interconnected coil system.

Delivery experiences differ significantly. Casper arrives compressed in a box for DIY setup, ideal for apartment dwellers or those wanting immediate gratification. Saatva includes complimentary white glove delivery with professional setup and old mattress removal, though scheduling requires coordination.

For durability, Saatva's steel coil construction typically outlasts all-foam mattresses. Owners report 10+ years of support from Saatva, while foam mattresses often develop body impressions after 7-8 years. Both offer industry-standard warranties, but Saatva's construction suggests better long-term value despite higher upfront cost.

The Casper vs Saatva decision ultimately depends on sleep position, budget, and personal preferences. Side sleepers on a budget will appreciate Casper's pressure relief and value pricing. Back sleepers, stomach sleepers, heavier individuals, and those prioritizing temperature control should invest in Saatva's superior support and breathability.

faq.q: Is Saatva worth the extra cost compared to Casper?
faq.a: Yes, for most sleepers. Saatva's dual-coil construction offers superior durability, temperature regulation, and edge support that justify the higher price. The included white glove delivery adds $200+ in value. However, budget-conscious side sleepers who prioritize motion isolation may find Casper's performance adequate at half the price.

faq.q: Which mattress is better for side sleepers, Casper or Saatva?
faq.a: Casper typically performs better for side sleepers under 200 lbs due to its pressure-relieving foam layers that cushion shoulders and hips. Saatva's Plush Soft option also works well for side sleepers, particularly those over 200 lbs who need more support, but the Luxury Firm and Firm models may create pressure points.

faq.q: Do Casper and Saatva mattresses sleep hot?
faq.a: Saatva sleeps significantly cooler due to its coil-on-coil construction that allows constant airflow. Casper retains more heat as all-foam mattresses naturally trap body warmth, though its perforated foam layers help somewhat. Hot sleepers should choose Saatva or consider Casper's hybrid models instead of the all-foam Original.

faq.q: How long do Casper and Saatva mattresses last?
faq.a: Saatva mattresses typically last 10-12 years due to durable steel coil construction, while Casper's all-foam design averages 7-9 years before developing significant body impressions. Both come with comparable warranties, but Saatva's construction materials inherently resist sagging longer than foam.

faq.q: Which has better edge support, Casper or Saatva?
faq.a: Saatva offers substantially better edge support thanks to its foam-encased perimeter and innerspring construction, providing 8-9 inches of usable surface area. Casper's all-foam edges compress more easily under weight, reducing the usable sleep surface to about 5-6 inches from the edge. This matters most for couples maximizing mattress space or those who sit on the bed's edge.

meta_title: Casper vs Saatva (2026): Which Mattress Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Casper vs Saatva mattresses. We break down construction, support, temperature regulation, and value to help you pick the right mattress for your sleep style.
